messages.author: Alan S
messages.text: Hey guys, thank you for the settings to connect to the phoenix public channel... Im able to talk with everyone there, but for some reason at distances over a mile I can not communicate from my first client/node to the second one (moms lora to alans lora). any ideas what i can do to resolve this?
messages.content_html: Hey guys, thank you for the settings to connect to the phoenix public channel... Im able to talk with everyone there, but for some reason at distances over a mile I can not communicate from my first client/node to the second one (moms lora to alans lora). any ideas what i can do to resolve this?

messages.author: Sonny AA7K
messages.text: Just moved a couple of my devices (Wio Tracker L1 Pro and Wis Block (4631)) onto 'core from 'tastic. I am in Queen Creek, and despite seeing a number of node placements on the map, I have seen ZERO actual traffic or devices online. I'm in the process of building a solar repeater around the Wis Block and should be able to get it up around 30 ft. AGL. If anyone is in the SE Valley, I'd love to join forces. I have not been happy with the usability on the 'tastic side and what I've read/heard about Core sounds appealing. I'll probably stay at it for a while. I don't mind being in early. I'm also a Ham (AA7K). I'll be at the Scottsdale Springfest next Saturday (14th) if anyone else is around. I'll be wearing a black cap with gold emroidery of my call sign. If you see me, say hello Feel free to ping me here as well.

I have not been happy with the usability on the 'tastic side and what I've read/heard about Core sounds appealing. I'll probably stay at it for a while. I don't mind being in early.
